agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"OrderFulfillmentWorker","to":"RabbitMQ queue `order.fulfillment`","status":"risk","issue":"RabbitMQ queues have not been provisioned. Infrastructure team has not been notified. The worker will fail to connect on deploy."},{"from":"OrderFulfillmentWorker","to":"RabbitMQ queue `order.fulfillment.dlq`","status":"risk","issue":"Dead-letter queue also unprovisioned. Failed messages will have nowhere to route, risking silent message loss or worker crash loops."},{"from":"Upstream producer","to":"RabbitMQ queue `order.fulfillment`","status":"broken","issue":"No producer has been identified or implemented. The worker consumes from a queue that nothing is writing to. The integration contract between producer and consumer is undefined."}],"contract_compliance":"The original spec said 'process orders asynchronously' but did not prescribe RabbitMQ, specific queue names, or new environment variables. The implementation made infrastructure decisions that were never validated with the user or infra team. This is scope expansion without sign-off.","data_flow_integrity":"Cannot verify end-to-end data flow. The producer side is unspecified — message schema, serialization format, ordering guarantees, and idempotency contracts are all undefined. A consumer without a defined producer contract is untestable in integration."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["Confirm structured logs include correlation/order IDs for tracing a single order through the async pipeline","Confirm DLQ events log the original message body and failure reason so on-call can diagnose without replaying"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"p99 latency exported — good starting point","gaps":["Queue depth metric (messages pending in order.fulfillment) — critical for detecting consumer lag","DLQ depth metric — rising DLQ depth is the #1 signal something is wrong","Consumer throughput (messages processed/sec)","Error rate metric (failures/sec) for alerting","Consumer redelivery count to detect poison-pill messages"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No mention of distributed trace propagation. If the upstream producer starts a trace, the worker must extract and continue it. Without this, async order processing is a black hole in traces."]},"debugging_capability":"Structured logging and a health endpoint are good foundations, but an on-call engineer at 3am needs: (1) a way to inspect what's stuck in the DLQ, (2) a way to replay or purge DLQ messages, and (3) dashboards showing queue depth trends. None of these are mentioned."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"RabbitMQ connection drops","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Implement reconnection with exponential backoff. Without it, the worker dies on transient network issues and requires manual restart."},{"scenario":"Poison-pill message (malformed payload that always fails)","handling":"partial","recommendation":"DLQ routing exists, which is good. But add a max-retry limit (e.g., 3 attempts) with backoff before DLQ routing. Without it, a bad message can spike CPU and error rates before finally dead-lettering."},{"scenario":"RabbitMQ is completely unavailable at startup","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Health endpoint should report degraded/unhealthy. Worker should retry connection on startup rather than crash immediately, or the orchestrator (k8s) will CrashLoopBackOff."},{"scenario":"DLQ queue is full or unavailable","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"If the DLQ itself is unreachable, the worker has no failure outlet. Define behavior: nack and requeue? Log and drop? This must be explicit."},{"scenario":"Duplicate message delivery (at-least-once semantics)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"RabbitMQ provides at-least-once delivery. The worker must be idempotent — processing the same order twice should not double-charge or double-ship. No idempotency strategy is mentioned."}],"retry_config":"Not specified. Must define: max retries, backoff strategy, which errors are retryable vs. terminal.","timeout_config":"Not specified. Must define: message processing timeout (what if fulfillment hangs for 10 minutes?), connection timeout, and consumer prefetch/QoS to prevent memory exhaustion.","circuit_breakers":"Not present. If the worker calls downstream services (inventory, shipping), circuit breakers are needed to prevent cascade failures.","graceful_degradation":"Not defined. What happens to incoming orders if the worker is down? Are messages retained in RabbitMQ with TTL? Is there a fallback synchronous path?"},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":false,"rollback_plan":"No rollback plan defined. Key questions: Can the queues be drained safely on rollback? What happens to in-flight messages if the worker is removed? Is the producer change deployed independently — if so, rollback requires coordinating two services.","monitoring_coverage":"Partial. p99 latency and health endpoint exist. Missing: queue depth alerting, DLQ depth alerting, error rate alerting, consumer lag dashboards.","on_call_documentation":"None mentioned. On-call runbook needed covering: how to inspect DLQ, how to replay messages, how to manually drain the queue, what alerts mean and how to respond, escalation path to infra team for RabbitMQ issues.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":["RABBITMQ_URL — connection string to RabbitMQ cluster","FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME — dead-letter queue name"],"new_services":["RabbitMQ cluster (or new vhost/permissions if shared cluster exists)"],"new_queues_topics":["order.fulfillment — primary work queue","order.fulfillment.dlq — dead-letter queue"],"config_changes":["RabbitMQ exchange bindings and DLQ routing policy","Consumer prefetch/QoS settings","Queue TTL and max-length policies"],"infra_provisioning":["RabbitMQ queues and exchanges must be provisioned before deploy","Secrets management entry for RABBITMQ_URL","Network/firewall rules if RabbitMQ is in a separate network segment"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No database migrations mentioned. If order fulfillment status is tracked in a DB, migration safety was not assessed."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"RabbitMQ queues and exchanges are not provisioned and infrastructure team has not been notified","reason":"The implementation introduced two new queues (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) and a RabbitMQ dependency that were not in the original spec. Deploying without provisioned infrastructure will result in immediate failure. The infra team must provision queues, configure exchanges, set DLQ routing policies, and add RABBITMQ_URL to secrets management.","routed_to":"Coach K → Infrastructure team"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"New environment variables (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME) not registered in deployment configuration","reason":"These env vars must be added to the deployment manifests (k8s secrets, Helm values, or equivalent) before the service can start. Without them, the worker will crash on boot.","routed_to":"Coach K → Infrastructure team"},{"type":"integration_risk","issue":"No producer exists for the order.fulfillment queue","reason":"The worker consumes messages, but no component has been built or identified to publish messages to the queue. The message schema contract is undefined. This is half an integration — shipping it provides zero value and creates operational burden (a running service that does nothing).","routed_to":"Coach K → Shaq (implementation) and MJ (architecture)"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No queue depth monitoring, DLQ alerting, or on-call runbook","reason":"Without queue depth and DLQ depth alerts, the on-call engineer has no way to know if orders are piling up or failing silently. Without a runbook, they cannot diagnose or remediate at 3am. This service will be a black box in production.","routed_to":"Coach K → Pippen (will author runbook once infra is confirmed)"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Infrastructure gap assessment — queues are definitively unprovisioned","Environment variable requirements — clearly specified","Missing producer identification — no evidence of a publishing component"],"low_confidence_areas":["Internal retry/reconnection logic — would need to read actual code to verify","Idempotency handling — cannot confirm without seeing message processing logic","Downstream service dependencies — spec does not detail what fulfillment actually calls"],"assumptions":["The implementation description is accurate and complete","No pre-existing RabbitMQ infrastructure exists for this use case","The health endpoint checks RabbitMQ connectivity (not just HTTP liveness)"]}}
